channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Are they right? is it over? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Are they right? is it over?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:52 UTC

OP

Saw this on /pol/, what do you think from a business perspective?

I see some businesses are actually responding in a way which supports this already, like some small-cap QSR businesses just got chopped today. But at the same time it kinda just seems like chud doomer slop, like... if there's a power deficit somewhere in the economy then the fed just print more money to equalize it right? How's an empire going to fall in a couple of years lol

Early replies (first 3)

62191314
>>62191311
Almost like its on purpose inflation to wipe out wealth like ai taking drops, et cetera

62191330
>>62191314
What does wiping out wealth accomplish tho? doesn't it just make people more angry and give institutions less places to get a return on?

62191342
Mega keked at this post

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62191354
>>62191330
basically, there is a giant solid blob in the earth's mantle causing magnetic field disruption which effects the climate and crop growth yields. Thats it. I've spoon fed you the obvious.

The blob used to be uniform, now it splitting wihch means the worst of it is over and it slowly going back to its normal phases.

On top of this, the sun is in a declining hot phase. Summers will be cooler and winters more cold.

62191358
>>62191354
Makes sense, already adjusting my portfolio for this as we speak

62191360
So will the S&P 500 Vanguard ETF go up or down?

62191367
>>62191360
Oh my sweet sweet goycattle... I've got a couple of words to say about your diversified ETF

62191369
>>62191360
well, the magnetic field weakening means less births and babies, less people, less workers. Too much space radiation getting to the ground fucking with sperm counts. Making males infertile. Its why the birthrates decline world wide.

62191401
>>62191311
>saw this on /pol/
Stopped reading there. Nu/pol/ is never right.

62191431
>>62191401
its true though

62191449
I'm not going to sell anything - which means yes it is all true and there's going to be unprecedented misery and markets tanking.
This marks a change from me usually selling in anticipation of bad things, which of course means the bad thing never comes and I get left behind.

62191457
don't forget about apocalyptic solar flares and the imminent mega volcano eruptions
an asteroid will hit the earth too
global pandemics
and aliens of course

62191474
>>62191457
don't forget your mom's fat ass that makes giant fart bubbles of course

62191498
>>62191431
Fuck off retarded nigger

62191504
>>62191498
your reaction is very telling. omfg, its 100% true

62191513
>>62191311
>>62191314
>>62191330
>>62191342
>>62191354
>>62191369
>>62191431
You sound low T

62191519
>>62191513
you sound like a massive faggot

62191525
>>62191513
Was just asking a question really, if you can't ask questions then that explains why your personality never made it in the business world

item.title: He's right you know. Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC… — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
He's right you know. Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:52 UTC

OP

He's right you know.

Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC which are rare and nobody can predict.

Pull yourself by your bootstraps, contribute 30k a year into your investment account, and watch it grow. In 10 years you'll be over millionaire and earning 100k+ in interest per year. In another 10 years you'll have over 2.5 million.

The collapse of the US market means the collapse of the world, and by that point we'll all be dead anyways.

Here's a fact, The US has #1 GDP, 32 trillion. You can say this and that, but it is the worlds largest economy, and it does the most money trading in the entire world and is the dominant force over the world.

Early replies (first 3)

62191402
He's right except with BTC instead of Boomer stocks

62191409
fr btc is a more logical value of storage in a collapsing world

62191415
>>62191402
>>62191409
>I know more about money than a guy who has 150 billion USD.
>MFW

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62191456
>>62191399
yes i wouldnt be gambling on shitcoins aswell if it was 1960 (the time he grew up in). remember he called bitcoin rat poison out of sheer boomer seethe and jealousy. so fuck off with this out of touch garbage. also also you dont become a billionaire by being goodie two shoes le smart investor.

62191473
>>62191415
This but unironically

62191477
>>62191399
He was a rich kid at a time when Americas economy exploded. Anything he invested in would see massive gains. He was no acumen besides being in the right place at the right time

62191491
>>62191456
>ACK
Even the 5 year performance of BTC has been pitiful. Hes right yet again. 38% over 5 years, 7%.

Lol even the S&P 500 for the past 5 years averaged 14-16% gain. He's right, yet again.

Rat poison. Next stop BTC at 65K USD.

62191493
>>62191477
He probably was very decently smart with his investments to give the old fart some props, everything came in unison to print him as the richest guy at the time. Good luck doing that now tho.

62191495
>>62191399
>Pull yourself by your bootstraps, contribute 30k a year into your investment account...
I make 45k a year anon..

62191501
>>62191399
BUT ITS TOO SLOW!!!!!!
I DON'T WANT TO WAIT 10 YEARS FOR MONEY!!
I WANT IT NOW NOW NOW NOW NOW NOW !!!!!!!!!!!
I DON'T WANT TO WHEEL STOCKS EITHER!!!
IT TAKES TOO LONG GETTING $100 A WEEK FOR FREE!!!!!!!!!!!

62191508
>>62191495
Then work harder, start a business, find a different job, get certified, become an electrician, something.

If you're only getting part time money you probably will make more money doing instacart 10 hours a day 5 days a week than whatever you're doing now. You should be earning at least 80k a year in america and ideally 30k a year contributed.

62191509
>>62191491
Why do you choose the 5 years? I dont give a shit about bitcoin but its the best performing asset in history if you take its whole history.

62191516
Kek

62191522
>>62191516
Didnt it actually beat BTC doebiet

62191523
>>62191493
>>62191477
It was also a time when the only people in the stock market where fellow upper class white guys. The government also pumped his bags by getting rid of pension and making everyone buy his portfolio

62191526
>>62191522
That is the Berkshire/ btc chart my dude

item.title: CONSENSUS- DAY 1 — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
CONSENSUS- DAY 1

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:57 UTC

OP

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GKqBymcpl7M

Early replies (first 3)

62191407
>>62191404
ishant be watching

62191408
Where is Sergey?

62191423
>>62191408
sir, he is not listed on the agenda. But he has spoken at every consensus since, and including, the mainnet launch announcement
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oBjq-cV2BkI

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62191444
>>62191408
i analyzed the agenda and there is a suspicious gap in time, between 1:10pm and 1:45pm on the main stage. That is when i believe, at the moment, sergey will reveal himself to us and the world

62191471
>>62191444
checked and blessed be sergey of nazareth

62191544
>>62191471

item.title: So the whole DTCC building on XRP was bullshit? — 8 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So the whole DTCC building on XRP was bullshit?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:32 UTC

OP

So the whole DTCC building on XRP was bullshit?

>a collection of various L1's
>shared infrastructure, not picking any single project
>a 3 layer cake for all participants to use

Damn you biz. I was walking 10 feet tall today even though I'm just a fucking manlet. Add to this, fucking Schwartz dumping his bags. Had to watch this Hadera bullshit only to get buttfucked once more. thanks biz!

Early replies (first 3)

62190068
>>62189888

t. saw >>62189576 and got mad at the commentary on how link is COMPLETELY excluded. The DTCC announced months ago they will use Canton also for tokenization. They said the product using Chainlink will not be launched for over 15 FUCKING YEARS by which point it will be completely irrelevant technologically. COPE

62190089
>>62189888
>>62190068
So fcuking bullish xrp

62190900
>>62189888
Checked, turns out it was the whole dttc building on chainlink that turned out to be bullshit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62190995
>>62190900
they are building on chainlink - its a chainlink DTCC project. They're testing these other L1s together.

62191003
>>62189888
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62191239
>>62190068
fpbp
/thread

62191412
>>62190068
>the DTCC announced months ago they will use Canton also for tokenization
so you mean they will use chainlink indirectly? Lol. Canton is paying for subsidized chainlink services for their users

>https://www.canton.network/canton-network-press-releases/canton-network-and-chainlink-enter-into-strategic-partnership-to-accelerate-institutional-blockchain-adoption-#:~:text=The%20Canton%20Network%20and%20Chainlink%20have%20a,convergence%20of%20traditional%20and%20decentralized%20capital%20markets

62191446
>>62190995
wagmi bros

item.title: High energy costs means no mining which means BTC will hit 1 million by 2027. — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
High energy costs means no mining which means BTC will hit 1 million by 2027.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:56 UTC

OP

High energy costs means no mining which means BTC will hit 1 million by 2027. If you aren't going all in your fucking stupid.
Cap my post

Early replies (first 3)

62191438
>>62190865
300k by August

62191532
>>62190865
High energy costs means less disposable income means lower demand. Bitcoin 10k soon.

62191540
>>62191532
bitcoin is energy dumbass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

62191537

Try again in 4 years.

item.title: Why do bankers hate the Clarity ACT and Trump? — 2 replies — 2.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do bankers hate the Clarity ACT and Trump?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:16 UTC

OP

Why do bankers hate the Clarity ACT and Trump?

Early replies (first 3)

62190981
>>62190955
It seems it's the end of crypto as an investment, with stablecoins becoming the only venue. So you can only get a few pennies for owning liquidity, while taking the risk of unlimited supply, code bugs and fraud. Bitcoin shows massive saturation and weakness as it falls down out of the rainbow chart lie, for example.

62191391
>>62190955
Bankers don't hate Trump.
Bankers hate stablecoin yields because they pay out better than them.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

OP

OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62191246
hell yeah im finna put new rims on my whip and buy my mama a house

62191387
hell yeah im finna whip my mama

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: DTCC Partners with Crypto Companies Ripple and Canton for Tokenized Securities — 30 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
DTCC Partners with Crypto Companies Ripple and Canton for Tokenized Securities

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-05 12:04 UTC

OP

They plan to launch in October 2026. The only companies they cite which are associated with specific non-stablecoin cryptos are Ripple (XRP), and Canton (CC (be careful if buying this, Coinbase and other exchanges have other listings of "CC" which are not the right one)).

Early replies (first 3)

62189586
>>62189576
Also ONDO. Sorry Ondobros, did not mean to disclude you.

62189594
>>62189576
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62189635
>>62189576
Not chainlink?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62189642
>>62189635
Guess not, nope not in there

62189644
>>62189635
Not according to the DTCC or their official press release on it.

62189757
>>62189576
BOOOOOOOM XRP. linkcels on suicide watch

62189839
>>62189635
the key here is to read carefully
>which is being designed ___with feedback and collaboration from more than 50 financial industry firms____
>___The firms____, part of the DTCC Industry Working Group
the idea has always been that chainlink is the one ***designing*** and not merely providing "feedback and collaboration"
you will see that chris barrett retweeting this dtcc announcement which he would not do if it was in anyway negative for chainlink
see dtcc talking about chainlink here to make it clear:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zxAgrSbwXZQ&t=630s

62189850
it's fucking over

62189855
>>62189839
Chris Barret has been fudding hard since 2017 he's OG bro

62189989
>>62189855
he came with rory from a failed project and got on board early with sergey

62189999
>>62189839
Bruh give me a BREAK. The DTCC said at Smartcon that they would not launch the Chainlink associated product for over 15 years. 15+ YEARS. Have you already forgotten? Why would they list service providers like Fireblocks but not Chainlink if they were using them? "Designing" is not collaboration but member broker dealers and financial service providers like FIS are? I don't even know who Chris Barrett is nor does he matter to the fact reality of the DTCC post itself which they published to their Twitter. This is SUPER COPIUM.

62190012
>>62189999
Checked as fuck. Sirgay stood really weirdly desperately like a lost baby vying for a photo op with Trump with everyone looking at him incredibly strangely and Trump didn't even turn to him. He spammed photos of himself and Brad and RFK was the top admin get he was able to reach at the VP dinner. Their entire business model is making announcements based on very little and hyping it to morons who they then dump on every single time. Zero percent chance CLL would be involved without making sure they were in the press release, especially if they are "designing" it.

62190017
>>62189839

62190067
>>62189999
mm.. then it's officially OVER
did i do it right?

62190080
>>62190067
It was over before it started. Link investors never understood their own tech or how it is a completely unneeded token on a random chain whose whole purpose is jewish rent seeking and entrenching friction into a system to cash in on the toll booth. Link is overvalued already and it is down like 80% from its ATH. There is no value prospect for it to be priced higher and everyone who buys it does so directly from CLL dumps after announcements of more price feeds that mean NOTHING to token holders.

62190096
>>62190080
wow i feel very bad now. i just want to thank you personally for keeping such a close eye on the project and warning people. you even went to the extent of watching smartcon interviews to find snippets of info to help form your analysis. thank you

62190097
>>62189999
>>62190080
Bullish at xrp

62190155
>>62189576
So over for link sirs

62190323
>>62189839
Also worth noting that almost every single one of those partners has done something with Chainlink if my memory serves me correctly. I’ve followed this project for awhile and recognize a lot of them.

62190854
>>62190096
It wasn't an arbitrary snippet that was ambiguous, retard. It was the most anticipated speech where Sirgay tried to hype it up and the DTCC rep corrected him and said it would be 15 years minimum. There were a dozen threads about it and it was ALL OVER every thread mentioning Smartcon on this board for the period. You are trying to act like me being right somehow matters less than it somehow being cringe or motivationally suspect that I know this fact because it requires special knowledge that is nevertheless publicly available and has been known by this whole board since the moment it happened. Why not just admit you were wrong and live in reality rather than trying to cope through playing weird and gay rhetoric games hoping you can dress up circumstantial representation of reality as being cringe in a way such that reality should not be considered?

62190876
it really is over for chainlink i sold my whole stack just now

62190891
>>62190876
unironically I've dropped half my stack while waiting for anything to happen. I guess the old predictions were right that they would just outlast the OGs until most people had lost their tokens

62190896
Fuck man... that's brutal....
Fuckin grateful i sold my linkstack long ago

62190928
Kek fuddies

62190929
>>62190891
yea, i sold my whole stack 15 times over the last few months, brutal

62190970
>>62190854
They were only talking about corporate actions in that case iirc

62190973
>>62189576
That is a list of Financial Market Participants, not infrastructure providers.
Almost all of them are literal Tradfi actors, and the ones that aren't (Backpack, Ondo, Payward, Bitwave, Circle) have heavy tradfi overlap.

>>62189576
Ripple Prime has nothing to do with XRP though.

>>62189999
>>62190854
>DTCC said at Smartcon that they would not launch the Chainlink associated product for over 15 years
You made this up.

62190988
>>62189576
It's over for LINK chuds

62191249
>>62190973
>Ripple Prime has nothing to do with XRP though.
Rent free baggie

62191362
>>62191249
Don't blame me, sport.

item.title: The LINK token has value — 64 replies — 30.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-05-05 10:50 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176989
>>62176914
What date is this video from?

62177088
>>62176295
Shartemis betray

62177113
>>62176284
>>62176298
>>62176344
>>62176415
>>62176477

62177436
>>62176989
2025. In the video they're expecting good things from 2026

62177765
>>62177436
I have expected good things for many years now. I'm still a believer but not a short term believer. Over the mid to long term I still think we'll win but it's definitely gonna be 10 years further down the line than we hoped for in 2018. In 2018 I'd said 2023 would be the year. But 3 years after that I don't see much in terms of price action, and who knows about actual adoption when the above video is still talking about trials, even if they are live trials.

62177825
>>62177765
at least they stopped calling them proof of concepts. bagholders really didn't like that term anymore. trials is also played out, so live trials it is. maybe they can call it "limited production usage" next, what do you think? would you like that term?

62177889
>>62176214
value like a new car has value, sure..

62177980
>>62176214
Only 273,000,000 LINK tokens left to dump before the team start dumping the reserve tokens. LOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOL. Get fucked stinkies

62177984
>>62177980
My ID is real fr no cap

62178008
>>62177825
Zach and that insufferable faggot, nullpackets, have been yapping about "live pre-production" for the past 2 years
it's truly hilarious

62178052
>>62178008
Ever since their breadcrumb boy at SWIFT stepped down there has been literally not a single shred of good news for the stinkers.

62178211
chainlink is the plumbing of the new finance world, and Link is the shit that flows through the pipes

on the other end of those pipes are link holders, whom sit with mouths open as receptacles to collect, drink, and shower in said shit

they do this with a smile on their face. why you ask? well, because, they are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62178228
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies

62178833
>>62178052
>seething at Tom

Cripple detected

62179742
>>62177984
your id has fboy in it, that is really funny
>>62178228
thanks for posting in the Chainlink thread
>>62177088
How? There's nothing for them to betray, they posted speculation then left
>>62177765
>I have expected good things for many years now.
That's good, meaning the company has been running strong for years, meaning the it's not a scam, meaning the number is going up over time

62181961
>>62176214
True, it's 9$, kek

62182305
>>62179742
I don't think it's a scam at all. I'm just unhappy with the price action. Otherwise, the company seems to be doing great.

62182805
>>62181961
and the number keeps going up!
>>62182305
that's your valid opinion!

62184151
Chainlink powering prediction markets, the numbers are going up

62184641
>>62176214
the revenue is increasing

62187729
>>62176214
>The LINK token has value
and that value is $8 to $9 dollars

62187773
>>62184641
not for stink holders tho

62187784
>>62184641
>>62182805
>>62184151
$9
kek baggie keep spamming your shitty videos

62188638
>>62176323
Holy copeeeee
LINKIES LOSSSST AHAHAHA MORE ADOPTION THAN MICROSOFT AND NVIDIA
50$ —> 6$

62189051
>>62177113

62190186
>>62187773
clarity act regulations, tokenization, security awareness, it's all changing in chainlinks favour at once
>>62187784
thanks! i will

62190231
>>62176910
These Jeet bot goy gold staking posts really fire me up bros

62190282
>>62190186
Are these the mechanisms that will allow chainlink token holders to sue for equity?

62190912
So... it was all fake huh?
Glad my gut instinct told me to sell

62191218
>>62190282
the only people who would try that are VC types who didn't buy early enough

item.title: >IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"… — 59 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"…

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-05 10:30 UTC

OP

>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!?
>NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER", I DIDNT MEAN LIKE THIS!!!!
>WHAT DO YOU MEAN YOURE TIRED!? THATS WHAT YOUR DESIGNATED 30 MINUTE BREAK IS FOR!!! WORK IS SUPPOSED TO BE AN 8 HOUR EXERCISE OF MISERY! THATS JUST HOW IT IS!!!
>D-DID YOU JUST SAY THAT IT DOESNT EFFECT YOUR EFFICIENCY!?!? DID YOU JUST POINT OUT THAT ITS 2AM AND THERE ARE NO GUESTS TO GIVE A SHIT?? THIS CANT BE HAPPENING!!!!!! THE POINT ISNT TO LOOK GOOD FOR CUSTOMERS, ITS TO UH- UM- PROVE YOUR A HARD WORKER!!!! BEING A HARD WORKER FOR ME IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING EVER AND I SAID YOU CANT SIT WHILE YOU WORK!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62181647
>>62181643
I look like this and say this

62181651
>>62181643
i'm in my late 30s (white collar job etc.) but i still remember how fucking retarded retail/service industry managers were. even the district manager "elites" were insufferably fucking dumb retards.

62181656
>>62181643
Yes. Because you don't want employees to develop bad habits

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62182189
>>62181895
I am lanky, I stay away from barbells. I stick with volleyball, swimming, table tennis, stuff like that.
My last office gig had standing desks. I would make resolutions to stand x hours every day just to rebound to sitting all the time again. In retail there is no chance of sitting which was awesome for me.

62182199
>>62181643
give me a job right now or I will kill you

62182202
Isn't standing healthier than sitting o algo?

62182298
>>62182202
Not if you’re an American.

62182391
>>62181643
Dude I think about this all the time at Amazon and it's one of the small indicators that this society is a retard dog and pony show. Sometimes I am waiting for 10 mins for my machine to be one and I think to myself "what if I could just SIT DOWN right now?" Heck my job and a lot of the other jobs could be done with a chair.

62182402
>>62181657
When I worked at a cannery in Alaska there was this 16 yo but he was very tall, maybe 6"6? Anyways he had a position of putting fish onto a conveyor belt and he was sitting on two stacked buckets and his long arms were doing all the work. Had a good pace and was doing good work. The manager waited till he got up and snatched those buckets with such rage I was surprised. Rarely saw him like that and it was usually for a good reason. The kid wondered what the issue was but we all knew that that's simply how it is when dealing in the real world. My manager doesn't even bust his ass physically at that job so idk what he was pissed about.

62182409
>>62181656
Are we animals? Are we children??? We can control what habits we develop just fine.

62182418
>>62182409
are we human or are we dancer

62182427
>>62182402
Idk, manager was mad he was sitting. What did the kid do after?
You can sit in private imo, just not in public.

62182448
>>62182409
Then how come 90% of people are poor?

62182449
>>62181824
Great, as soon as you slip up you're fired, thanks bye

62182461
>>62182449
I simply won't slip up. Brb it's time for my hourly bowel movement. Sucks for you LOL

And to think all you had to do was respect your wagies

62182475
>>62182427
What was the issue with him sitting? His pace was just the same and due to hi unusual height he was able to reach everything just fine. These were 16-24 hour shifts mind you, 100hr work weeks

62182497
Wait until the middle management shills ITT find out about Porn at Work Wednesdays

62182525
>>62182461
What gets me is when team leads make a big show when they don't have the power to write up anyone or even the fact that none of the shit is falling down on them. Some people just get off on being in "charge" of others.

62182537
>>62181666
>uses 3 rolls of toilet paper to make the seat level again
Not My Fucking Problem.

62182610
Man I always feel so comfy reading these threads from my work from home lake house, caught a big bass last Thursday while on long boring teams meeting from my dock

62182685
>>62182610
lol legit cope post

62183229
>>62181737
Lmao cope harder cuck

62184087
I think we should start banning employees from having caffeine too. It, like sitting, is too convenient and makes the work day too easy, am I right?

62184093
>>62181666
>Turns my 30 minutes toilet breaks into free leg workout
Sign me the fuck up

62184233
Why the fuck do blue collar workers get treated like shit compared to white collar ones?

I've worked all types of jobs (retail, warehouse, white collar office, etc.) and the double standards are insane. In my last warehouse job you literally could not be a single second over your break time. You could not even idle or take a breather if you finished your allotted work (because you were just faster/better at your job) so they find some other random shit for you to do. They penalize you for working harder.

Meanwhile in my current fake email job I can just leave my desk and go for a walk anytime I want. I can disappear for 30 mins to go to the grocery store. No one cares.

62185961
Fuck target wagies

62185969
>>62181734
that sounds like hell

62187378
>>62184233
You can disappear in your fake email job because your productivity doesn't need to be as rigorously monitored as the average blue collar worker. You're not trapped in a machine for 12 hours or stuck on an assembly line and need to make quotas. Your work doesn't produce anything of value, or you'd be getting your ass kicked from the moment you clock in. Just be glad you don't work for someone like Mr Jassy. Or you'd be getting your ass kicked and still producing nothing of value.

62188384
>>62185961
BASED BVLL

62188412
>>62184233
>>62187378
It depends on your boss in white collar though. I've had a boss who told me I couldn't read a book at my desk during downtime, even though I was in my own private office and no one could see me. Quit that job the next day and left them hanging for the monthly call.

62190691
>>62182016
>okay then give me my full hour long break as legally mandated so i can go home to get food

62190708
>>62182497
Board directors are gonna fuckin flippppp when they find out about Absolutely Cranking Your Shit In The Unisex Bathroom Thursdays

62191181
theres no reasons why cashiers shouldnt be able to sit at least when no customers are in line

item.title: RETURN OF THE KING — 22 replies — 15.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
RETURN OF THE KING

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-05 11:32 UTC

OP

LUNC just dropped a zero
None of you retards listened
You could of been rich
Instead you're all Sidelined for lunc szn

Early replies (first 3)

62188919
>>62188819
someone fucking post the vocaroo

62188994
>>62188819
ok, now zoom out
kek baggies

62189013
>>62188994
>t. buck broken linkie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62189037
>>62188994
>he didn't buy the Generational dip
Kek see you at $119

62189068
>>62188919
stinky linkies! Stiiiinky....

62189130
>>62189037
WAGMI

62190223
>>62188819
ok, where to buy?

can it be purchased in trust wallet?

62190249
>>62190223
Kraken, kucoin, uphold, binance, take your pick. There's several more exchanges but those are the big ones. You can open a non custodial wallet on Terra Station or Galaxy Station and stake what you buy.

62190275
>>62190249
binance wallet withdrawal for LUNC is under maintenance, I wanna buy it but I ain't keeping it there, is the maintenance gonna take that long?

62190308
>>62188919
>>62189068
https://vocaroo.com/1fk7klnZQdct

there you go LUNChads.

its time to revive /TLCG/ breads, the prophecy is nearing, lots of shitters left and only the faithful chads hold.

62190366
>>62190275
I personally don't have binance (I'm a burger) so I use kraken and until recently, uphold. I don't recommend uphold if you want to send LUNC anywhere, they don't have the capability to send it to other exchanges or wallets.

62190374
>>62188819
Crazy
I just grabbed at 0.00006, didn't expect it to go up.

I'll be happy at 1 penny though, maybe by eoy.

62190453
I bought this the night of the crash for the memes'sake of it. The threads were comfy, the memes were funny, and the drama was pure gold. Despite being in a bear market, the space still had lots of gems in it and people still had hope in the different narratives that were being pushed. Since 2024, I just about lost all hope and it's only gotten worse since then. The space has been raped for whatever it had left, and no one really gives a shit about crypto anymore because there is nothing to look forward to. All the narratives have been played out. and now we're dealing with jewish trade bots farming whatever liquidity is left. Outside of some random pumps here and there, I don't ever see crypto returning to what it once was desu. Even if lunc did a 10x (it won't), that shit won't be worth the 4 years it took to hold it for anyone holding a 10m make it stack. There's no way out of this hellhole. I'm so fucking depressed

62190506
>>62190453
>Even if lunc did a 10x (it won't), that shit won't be worth the 4 years it took to hold it for anyone holding a 10m make it stack
ngl I never thought about that but $10k isn’t shit. This would have to do a 100x minimum and there’s no fucking way that’s happening

62190510
>>62190308
yeahahhh!
>this glorious basterd innit!

62190520
>>62188819
I still believe figuring out how to create a algorithmic trust free stable coin is the holy grail of crypto and everything will gigamoon when we figure it out

62190550
>>62190520
Nigga you can just put your money into fucking tether if you want a stablecoin. Inventing an algorithmic stable coin pegged to the dollar is like building a cleaning robot that needs to shit, listen to mariachi music, and takes US dollars. IF I WANTED THAT ID JUST HIRE A MEXICAN.

62190656
>>62190453
>that shit won't be worth the 4 years it took to hold it for anyone holding a 10m make it stack

>sweating <$1,000 USD

oof and kek

62191183
>>62190656
I’m not sweating over $1000. I got my bag at ~00004 so I don’t really care. The point that I was making is that crypto was our last chance for making any significant amount of money that would free us from having to wage for the next 40 years, and now that’s no longer possible. The only people who have been making money from lunc are jews like gcr who had millions invested into it. The rest of us can basically go fuck off

62191184
>>62188819
>Saaaar my dead shitcoin will definitely pump sar! Please do the needful sar.

62191302
>>62191183
nobody is forcing you to stay with LUNC

just abandon crypto if its not working for you

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 171 replies — 9.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 171 • Updated: 2026-05-04 19:38 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

She would force him to take drugs like viagra so she could fuck him longer. She started buying him gifts which as sex toys including anal ones which she forced him to use and got him a position where she had direct control over him and he acted as her assistant. She also pegged him.

He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.

She was caught when police investigated her for ties to Trump and Epstein with her blackmailing the employee being unclassified.

62172413
>>62172412

62172414
>>62172413

62172420
>>62172413
post her tits are they big and nice?

62180263

62180643
>>62174634

I suggest that alternatively

>Makes up this story to recoup his losses

62180818

62180821

FFS leave her alone.


>>62176674

62180823

62180838

62180849

lol. they would 100% get tons of dates from asian men who want a blonde gf. but they are probably just picky bitches who want for a 10/10 western expat chad

62180851

Yeah, nobody else believes him either.

>saar my benis is so yuge that white bitch demand it from me saar

>ties to Trump and Epstein
Then why was she going after a guy of legal age?

62180859
>>62180849
>they are probably just picky bitches
Yep. I lived in Taiwan for most of the last 20 years, and the two things every white male always heard from Western female expats was (1) they can't get dates because all the white guys have yellow fever, and (2) "eww the local guys have tiny dicks and don't make enough money to date me!"

In all fairness the Taiwanese guys do have tiny dicks tho.

62180991
>>62180975
not funny

62180994
>>62172412
THIS MAN IS NOT INDIAN HE IS PAKISTAN PLEASE STOP WITH THE SPREADING OF MISINFORMATION IT IS VERY DAMAGING

62181004
>>62180991
Get sprayed, bug

62181132
>>62175811
Looking forward to the defamation lawsuit

62183872
>>62180994
All brown fuck off.

62183970
>>62183872
He's not indian, he's Nepalese.

62184054
I have not had a extensive look into the story yet.
But here is a funny aspect.

Her name Hajdini
originates from:
The surname Hajdini is of Albanian origin, rooted in the Balkans,
It is derived from the word hajdin,
which historically referred to an outlaw or brigand

They were outlaws used in europe to fight Arabs who wanted to conquer Europe
(A different way to write it is Hajduk
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hajduk
The etymology of the word hajduk is unclear. One theory is that hajduk was derived from the Turkish word haidut or haydut 'bandit',
... because the Turkish word haidut or haydut is adapted from the Hungarian hajtó or hajdó
Other spellings in English include ajduk, haydut, haiduk, haiduc, hayduck, and hayduk.

It is interesting because aren't there posts where she allegedly refers
to his arab origin ...

62184088
>>62180851
>Yeah, nobody else believes him either.
It would at least be interesting to ask this question, and have a look at who profits from throwing her out.

62185236

FAN
I
C
T
I
O
N

62186927
>>62176674
Harder, mommy
t. 42 years old faggot who is slightly depressed right now because milfs are around my age or younger

62186965
>>62174807
These western white women don't tell you how old they are and how much they weight. Imagine you're a chang in your 20's and doing pretty well in life, why would you date a white, overweight, old, feminist borderline personality unsufferable bitch with daddy issues? You'd rather date a traditional feminine and obedient 18 years old chinkette. These sluts blame the Asian men as feminine and shy, lol

62186984
I would let her do whatever she wanted to me

62186988

62186993

62187412

correct information, but I should have read the complete thread first.
>>62174634

62187447
>>62172902
It’s because he is a fucking liar that was about to get fired and wants a payout.

62187457
I'd like to sample her birdie num nums if you know what I mean

62188179
>>62172412
You guys are still posting about this stupid shit?

* the complainant was a poo
* the complainant was incompetent at his job
* the complainant realized he was about to get fired
* the complainant made it all up so he could try to extort a settlement out of the company
* the company figured out before the news even hit the media that it was this poo's fantasy ravings
* the femoid didn't even have control over the poo's bonus as they worked under completely different managers
* the poo has deleted all of his social media
* incidentally, the poo is somehow related to the former ruling family of Nepal whom everyone apparently hates

62188941
>>62172412

62188965

62186859

62187145

62187475
it's not enough stinkies ruined their own lives
they have to ruin everyone else's as well

62187946

62188050

62188119

62190631

62190633
I think with bitcoin pumping to 83k as a bull trap we may break over $10 briefly. Hopefully trim my realized loss down to 20k.
>t. 5200 stinky cost basis of 12.48
Couldn’t imagine being more underwater or spending years holding. I started accumulating a year ago and got too excited. I’ll be buying a fresh bag of sub $5 Stinkies after the fallout while slurping more PMs. In fact, the whole fuckin process I had to deal with of trying to get my coins off my Ledger on a MacBook I hadn’t used before was a great reminder of how fucking stupid and incompatible to is shit is with sovereignty. I’ll spare the details. All I wanted was a pumperino to exit and go off grid, but I’ve learned a valuable lesson and am grateful it was 1 year of sunken cost with no returns. I don’t even know if I’ll slurp at this point, we’ll see

62191235
Sergey likes bitcoin, because he knows bitcoin treasury and BTC defi needs chainlink

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 71 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-05-05 04:03 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62185888
>>62185829
>Like it was a choice
As the poet once said "If you decide not to decide you still have made a choice."

62186168
>>62185702
Which is weird right, because not 50 years ago "full employment" meant that EVERYONE who wanted a job had one and was also government policy across much of the western world

62186171
>>62184286
It's been like this for over 20 years. We haven't had a real president since Bush.

62186720
>>62184556
>$90k for a "management role"
>bragging about IQ

62186790
Good, majority of people are replaceable and if the evolution of market makes them not needed, they need to perish. This is capitalism and eugenetics working as intended.

62186797
>>62186790
>majority of people
everyone is replaceable.

62187055
>>62186168
>"full employment" meant that EVERYONE who wanted a job had one
It has never meant that though. It was obvious as soon as unemployment numbers started being collected that there was a baseline level of unemployment that was unavoidable without resorting to slavery.
Also "wanted" is doing a lot of heavy lifting in your claim there without some denotation as to the intensity of the "want". Either you knew this but hoped nobody else would notice, in which case you're a disengenuous little shit, or you didn't notice it in which case you're at least moderately stupid.

62187058
>>62186790
Cemeteries are full of men who were certain they were irreplaceable.

62187063
>>62184556
My best friend worked in multiple industries for said cross domain knowledge, but not for a sad 90k in Muttmerica, but 200k in Europe (equivalent to 800k in Muttmerica, but something like 70k net in socialist Europe). Recently unemployed due to the IT company going bust, he exhausted his network, zero job interviews.

>>62186790
Clearly the people on top are not replaceable due to their superhuman skills.
Don't even try, I have a company doing something specialized for luxury real estate. My mother was a social worker dealing with retardation, her clients were way more skilled and grown up than mine are today.

62187071
>>62184286
Men are checking out of society at an alarming rate and the trend will only continue.

62187078
>>62187071
And that is very welcomed by everyone who is rich. Less competition, more resources, more fun in life. Check out and get marginalized in a pod. Die early, unnoticed.

62187106
>>62184286
Just say you're trans. Once hired you can say you're de transing. Should give you a job in 2 weeks.

62187112
>>62187106
Relevant already a few years ago

62187120
>>62187063
>Clearly the people on top are not replaceable due to their superhuman skills.
i work at a fortune 10 company. the board fired the CFO and CEO after these "superhumans" blew up the price by being retarded and incompetent. they were replaced in a matter of months. no one is irreplaceable. business talent is largely a retarded meme.

62187124
>>62184366
Why can’t you spell? Are you drunk because you didn’t get the job at the vapourware company?

62187126
>>62187120
**blew up the stock price

62188258
>>62185827
die-versity hire, they're probably looking for a nig of some sort. Probably not an indian though or else they'd have filled it lol.

Or perhaps you're flagged on some sort of secret 'no-hire' list for being edgy online or whatever. My workplace has a secret 'do not rehire' list that the employees aren't supposed to know about, and the way we found out was that the woman who resigned was flagged for it, she was one of the last people us coworkers would have predicted was tagged for it.

62188279
>>62186790
if the governments of the world totally shut off immigration to suit this reality, i'd not be concerned, the problem is they want to import an endless legion of turd world subhumans into the west at the same time as there's no real jobs left for the citizens. Whilst promoting their globohomo bloodsports in Ukraine and the ME.

>come white man, enlist! we only let you get the gig when we need goyim to die for lsraeI! heres you're chance!

62188591
>>62184289
>skill issue.
reddit post.

62188606
>>62184286
>they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds
By the way, they totally could figure it out in one interview. But they string you along for 3+ interviews because they think it's funny.

62188643
>>62184286
Just learn to prompt

62188765
>>62184556
>Reality is an IQ test, what do you think your IQ is?
So my boss must be smarter than me...that's why im retiring in 3 years and that fucker will be slaving until 80

62188796
>>62186790
Which is why those ideologies are either dumb or evil tempting.

62188815
>>62188279
It’s funny how you mix Ukraine with some jewish conspiracy.

62188832
>>62188796
No, communism is evil and everyone who promotes communism is a soulless monster.

62188926
>>62188815

Israeli IP

62188963
you laughed at le trade meme
hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702
That all just sounds like bullshit

>>62187055

62190516
>>62185827

capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way
